{{nihongo|'''Nakayama Shrine'''|中山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

{'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:中山神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11364249}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=中山神社 | 画像=[[File:Nakayama shrine.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町信友697 | 位置={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=中山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''中山神社'''（なかやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失しており、それ以前の記録は存在しない。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}12年（{{jalink|1569年|Q6653}}）に再建。以後{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}、{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}、{{jalink|延宝|Q1145735}}年間に社殿の造営、再建の記録がある。 {{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}は{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で中山神社を移転再建したさい、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称したという。現在の中山神社は元の位置に再建した神社と推測される。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} *:{{jalink|建御名方神}}の説もある === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやましんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]]

{'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:中山神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11364249}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=中山神社 | 画像=[[File:Nakayama shrine.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町信友697 | 位置={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=中山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''中山神社'''（なかやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失しており、それ以前の記録は存在しない。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}12年（{{jalink|1569年|Q6653}}）に再建。以後{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}、{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}、{{jalink|延宝|Q1145735}}年間に社殿の造営、再建の記録がある。 {{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}は{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で中山神社を移転再建したさい、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称したという。現在の中山神社は元の位置に再建した神社と推測される。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} *:{{jalink|建御名方神}}の説もある === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやましんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町信友697 | 位置={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=中山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''中山神社'''（なかやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失しており、それ以前の記録は存在しない。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}12年（{{jalink|1569年|Q6653}}）に再建。以後{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}、{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}、{{jalink|延宝|Q1145735}}年間に社殿の造営、再建の記録がある。 {{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}は{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で中山神社を移転再建したさい、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称したという。現在の中山神社は元の位置に再建した神社と推測される。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} *:{{jalink|建御名方神}}の説もある === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやましんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]

{'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:中山神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11364249}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=中山神社 | 画像=[[File:Nakayama shrine.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町信友697 | 位置={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=中山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''中山神社'''（なかやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失しており、それ以前の記録は存在しない。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}12年（{{jalink|1569年|Q6653}}）に再建。以後{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}、{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}、{{jalink|延宝|Q1145735}}年間に社殿の造営、再建の記録がある。 {{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}は{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で中山神社を移転再建したさい、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称したという。現在の中山神社は元の位置に再建した神社と推測される。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} *:{{jalink|建御名方神}}の説もある === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやましんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}

{'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:中山神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11364249}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=中山神社 | 画像=[[File:Nakayama shrine.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町信友697 | 位置={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=中山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''中山神社'''（なかやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失しており、それ以前の記録は存在しない。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}12年（{{jalink|1569年|Q6653}}）に再建。以後{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}、{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}、{{jalink|延宝|Q1145735}}年間に社殿の造営、再建の記録がある。 {{jalink|諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11631934|諏訪神社}}は{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で中山神社を移転再建したさい、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称したという。現在の中山神社は元の位置に再建した神社と推測される。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山彦命}} *:{{jalink|建御名方神}}の説もある === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやましんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}

Torii and worship hall | ???={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}} Shimoyoneda-ch? Nobutomo 697 | ??={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | ??={{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | ??={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}?former {{jalink|G?-sha|Q134917316}} | ??=unknown | ??= | ??=first Sunday of April | ??= | ??= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Nakayama Shrine''' (????????) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine of the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}} (the other candidate shrine is {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|Suwa Shrine}}). === History === Its founding date is unknown. It was destroyed by fire in wartime during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era, and no records remain from before that. It was rebuilt in Eiroku 12 ({{jalink|1569|Q6653}}). After that, records of shrine construction and rebuilding exist for the {{jalink|Keich?|Q1328185}}, {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}}, and {{jalink|Enp?|Q1145735}} eras. It is said that {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|Suwa Shrine}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{jalink|Kota Genryu}}, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, then enshrined {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:???????? ????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}

It is said that {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|Suwa Shrine}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{jalink|Kota Genryu}}, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, then enshrined {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:???????? ????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}

Nobutomo 697 | ??={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | ??={{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | ??={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}?former {{jalink|G?-sha|Q134917316}} | ??=unknown | ??= | ??=first Sunday of April | ??= | ??= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Nakayama Shrine''' (????????) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine of the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}} (the other candidate shrine is {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|Suwa Shrine}}). === History === Its founding date is unknown. It was destroyed by fire in wartime during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era, and no records remain from before that. It was rebuilt in Eiroku 12 ({{jalink|1569|Q6653}}). After that, records of shrine construction and rebuilding exist for the {{jalink|Keich?|Q1328185}}, {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}}, and {{jalink|Enp?|Q1145735}} eras. It is said that {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|Suwa Shrine}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{jalink|Kota Genryu}}, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, then enshrined {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:???????? ????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}

Nobutomo 697 | ??={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | ??={{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | ??={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}?former {{jalink|G?-sha|Q134917316}} | ??=unknown | ??= | ??=first Sunday of April | ??= | ??= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Nakayama Shrine''' (????????) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine of the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}} (the other candidate shrine is {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|Suwa Shrine}}). === History === Its founding date is unknown. It was destroyed by fire in wartime during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era, and no records remain from before that. It was rebuilt in Eiroku 12 ({{jalink|1569|Q6653}}). After that, records of shrine construction and rebuilding exist for the {{jalink|Keich?|Q1328185}}, {{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}}, and {{jalink|Enp?|Q1145735}} eras. It is said that {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|Suwa Shrine}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{jalink|Kota Genryu}}, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, then enshrined {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:???????? ????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}[[Category:qq]]

It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District}} (the other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine}}). === History === Its founding date is unknown. It was destroyed by fire in wartime during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} era, and no records remain from before that. It was rebuilt in Eiroku 12 ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653}}). After that, records of shrine construction and rebuilding exist for the {{ill|lt=Keich?|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}}, {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}, and {{ill|lt=Enp?|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} eras. It is said that {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kota Genryu|UNKNOWN}}, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|UNKNOWN}}, then enshrined {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:???????? ????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is said that {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kota Genryu|1=UNKNOWN}}, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, then enshrined {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:???????? ????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii and worship hall | ???={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimoyoneda-ch? Nobutomo 697 | ??={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | ??={{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} | ??={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}?former {{ill|lt=G?-sha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | ??=unknown | ??= | ??=first Sunday of April | ??= | ??= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Nakayama Shrine''' (????????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}} (the other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}). === History === Its founding date is unknown. It was destroyed by fire in wartime during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era, and no records remain from before that. It was rebuilt in Eiroku 12 ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653|1=1569}}). After that, records of shrine construction and rebuilding exist for the {{ill|lt=Keich?|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}}, {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}, and {{ill|lt=Enp?|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} eras. It is said that {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kota Genryu|1=UNKNOWN}}, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, then enshrined {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:???????? ????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It was destroyed by fire in wartime during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era, and no records remain from before that. It was rebuilt in Eiroku 12 ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653|1=1569}}). After that, records of shrine construction and rebuilding exist for the {{ill|lt=Keich?|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}}, {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}, and {{ill|lt=Enp?|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} eras. It is said that {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kota Genryu|1=UNKNOWN}}, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, then enshrined {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:???????? ????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:中山神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11364249}} == Japanese content == {{?? | ??=Nakayama Shrine | ??=[[File:Nakayama shrine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | ???={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimoyoneda-ch? Nobutomo 697 | ??={{Coord|35|28|2.48|N|137|5|8.66|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} | ??={{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} | ??={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}?former {{ill|lt=G?-sha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | ??=unknown | ??= | ??=first Sunday of April | ??= | ??= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Nakayama Shrine''' (????????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}} (the other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}). === History === Its founding date is unknown. It was destroyed by fire in wartime during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era, and no records remain from before that. It was rebuilt in Eiroku 12 ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653|1=1569}}). After that, records of shrine construction and rebuilding exist for the {{ill|lt=Keich?|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}}, {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}, and {{ill|lt=Enp?|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} eras. It is said that {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kota Genryu|1=UNKNOWN}}, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, then enshrined {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:qq]]

It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}} (the other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}). === History === Its founding date is unknown. It was destroyed by fire in wartime during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era, and no records remain from before that. It was rebuilt in Eiroku 12 ({{ill|lt=1569|Q6653|WD=Q6653|1=1569}}). The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:qq]]

It is said that {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11631934|WD=Q11631934|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}} moved and rebuilt Nakayama Shrine by order of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kota Genryu|1=UNKNOWN}}, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, then enshrined {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} and renamed it Suwa Shrine. The current Nakayama Shrine is thought to be a shrine rebuilt at the original location. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} *:There is also a theory identifying this deity as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]]
